evidence_summary: Graph neural networks can leverage graph structure to enhance node classification performance with scarce labels by encoding the graph structure directly using a neural network model.
Conditioning the model on the adjacency matrix of the graph allows it to distribute gradient information from the supervised loss and learn representations of nodes both with and without labels.
A scalable approach for semi-supervised learning on graph-structured data can be achieved through an efficient variant of convolutional neural networks that operate directly on graphs.
